title: ranunculus auricomus subsp. envalirensis
intro: Ranunculus auricomus subsp. envalirensis, commonly known as the lesser celandine or fig buttercup, is a subspecies of the Ranunculus auricomus plant, a type of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its alkaloids and essential oils. The health benefits of Ranunculus auricomus subsp. envalirensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, although its use should be consulted with a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and allergic reactions.
